codeReview.summary: The feature implementation matches the requested scope: successful Riot ID searches are trimmed, routed into /player/{gameName}#{tagLine}, persisted locally as deduplicated recent lookups, re-runnable from the recent-lookup UI, and prior player-specific dashboard state is cleared before loading a different player. The direct player-route auto-load path and incomplete-input guard are also present, and the new saved-lookup helper has focused unit coverage for trimming, dedupe/recency, invalid storage data, incomplete identities, and list size limits.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The web-feature-worker skill still treats `.factory/init.sh` as a normal setup artifact, but this Windows mission host cannot execute that POSIX shell script directly. The reviewed worker transcript records a failed attempt and a workaround instead of a documented Windows-safe path.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton/handoff for worker session 2e6b84a7-4125-4ec7-a8eb-addcf05d0183 notes `.factory/init.sh` could not run on Windows and suggests documenting a Windows-safe invocation; repo environment docs in `.factory/library/environment.md` already note the machine is Windows.

summary: Reviewed feature search-saved-lookups-and-route-synchronization by inspecting mission metadata, the completed worker handoff/transcript, commit 8a313cf, and the touched dashboard/helper files. I found the implementation consistent with the feature description and expected behavior, with no blocking or non-blocking code defects in scope; the only notable shared-state gap is missing Windows guidance for `.factory/init.sh` in the worker setup path.
